title: Siamese Neural Network for User Authentication in Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) for Wearable Applications

biometric.body_location: right arm (palmaris longus and extensor digitorum)

method.training_strategy: Keras framework, Adam optimizer, Step decay learning rate, Bit quantization for hardware deployment

evaluation.context: Achieved on the training dataset of 80 individuals and maintained during hardware deployment

evaluation_protocol: 80 subjects for training (with 25% validation) and 20 subjects for testing; binary classification (same person vs different person).

reported_limitations: Relatively low accuracy of EMG signals
Limited hardware resources on edge devices
Requirement for multimodal biosignals to further increase accuracy

key_contribution: Hardware implementation of a lightweight Siamese network on FPGA for EMG authentication
Integration of MODWT for efficient time-frequency feature extraction on edge devices
main_strength: Few-shot learning capability allowing new users without full retraining
High hardware efficiency with only 6835 parameters
main_weakness: Tested on a single specific motion (fist clenching)
Lack of evaluation in uncontrolled/real-world environments
Absence of active spoofing attack tests
insights.notes_for_review: The paper focuses heavily on the hardware optimization (FPGA/HLS) rather than the biometric robustness across different activities.
